attrs.statement: Contract processing must remain schema-first.
Contract source is limited to files declared in PATHS and contracticalSourceBlocks.
Active app PATHS must contain filesystem facts only and use canonical Dir/File/Glob/Pattern leaf names.
Active app contracts must declare PATH_SURFACES to separate authority/lifecycle from PATHS.
Active app contracts must not publish duplicate PATHS aliases for the same concrete filesystem path.
Active app contracts must not duplicate core-owned wiki/SSOT mechanics outside the permitted app wiki data surface.
Contract MUST NOT contain runtime state or mutable data.
Generated artifacts (.frida/contract/profiles/, .frida/contract/AGENTS.md, .frida/contract/docs/) must not be manually edited.
Temporary files must reside in .temp/ directories.
Agent MUST NOT directly modify .frida/contract/internal/ contents.
Agents MUST NOT directly access `.frida/inbox/**`; inbox is protected inbound/SSOT surface.
Every agent run MUST produce a job report in .frida/reports/.
Visual overlay generation MUST be deterministic.
Each zone MUST have a contractical AGENTS.md at its agentsPath.
Template hash manifest must validate; drift returns non-zero.
Bootstrap is manual-only and must not be invoked transitively by other commands.
Demo bootstrap is explicit-only and zero-deploy-only; full bootstrap defaults to warm reconcile.
Explicit bootstrap component-restore re-run must not duplicate existing components.
Only one active session token per branch.
VALIDATE_ANTITASK phase MUST run before EXECUTE. Run report MUST include verification.antitask per FRIDA_ANTITASK_SPEC.report_proof.
Component-level contracts MUST declare all canonical component_* boundary sections.
Mounted child boundary refs and continuation/return targets MUST resolve when declared.
Root boundaries MAY omit parent_boundaryRef; non-root boundaries MUST resolve a valid parent boundary when required by the model.
If local_role=host_root is present, it MUST be unique, MUST omit parent_boundaryRef, and MUST act as the single depth=0 anchor for the host baseline.
mounted_child_boundaryRefs MUST be unique, MUST NOT self-target, and MUST NOT create ambiguous child drill-in declarations.
component_output_interface exit entries MUST include required outcome semantics.
continue requires target_boundaryRef, return requires return_target_boundaryRef, and exit forbids both.
Parent specification view MUST NOT inline mounted child internals; child expansion requires explicit drill-in.
component_domain_blocks projection MUST remain selector-driven on explicit projection_domains declarations and MUST NOT use heuristics.
component_shared_refs MUST remain dependency-only and MUST NOT carry ownership, hierarchy, entry, or exit semantics.
Authoritative component-contract examples MUST remain runtime-free; viewer/session demo state is allowed only in explicit fixture-only reference surfaces.
Legacy component-boundary section names are forbidden in active contract definitions.
Mixed legacy/new component-boundary namespace usage is forbidden in active component contract definitions.
If a top-level app entity cannot sustain canonical name -> boundary -> refs coupling, it must be demoted or split rather than retained as an ambiguous boundary.
Visualization semantics MUST remain boundary-first, with YAML depth secondary and exits sourced from component_output_interface.

attrs.text: Any active contract entity MUST be declared exactly once. Duplicate declarations and semantic aliases are forbidden.
Visual artifacts are derived overlays and must not become source-of-truth contract state.
Visual artifacts are refreshed only by explicit visual commands.
Same contract input MUST produce identical visual overlay output.
Temporary files MUST reside in .temp/ directories and never in working tree root.
Temporary artifacts are ephemeral to a single session; no cross-session dependency allowed.
Error conditions MUST be explicit. Silent failures are forbidden.
Agent MUST NOT exceed the scope defined by its profile security permissions.
Contract is a build-time/design-time artifact. Runtime state MUST NOT be stored in contract.
Only one agent session token may be active per branch at any time.
Explicit bootstrap component-reconcile/component-restore mode is idempotent and restores or normalizes selected components without duplication.
Contract bootstrap MUST run only via explicit bootstrap CLI invocation and MUST NOT be auto-invoked by other commands.
Default contract bootstrap mode is warm reconcile for an existing Frida deployment; missing Frida markers must HALT with explicit cold/demo redirect.
Demo bootstrap mode is explicit-only and zero-deploy-only.
Warm and cold-engine bootstrap modes MUST NOT modify app-contract files.

Agents MUST NOT use `.frida/inbox/**` as a general-purpose work surface. Direct edits are allowed only under `.frida/inbox/app-contract/**` through `FRIDA_INTERFACE_UPDATE_APP_BY_SPEC` or `FRIDA_INTERFACE_UPDATE_APP_BY_CODE` using the interface-scoped `app_contract_editor` profile, or through `FRIDA_INTERFACE_TARGET_TOOLCHAIN_UPGRADE` using `frida_toolchain_upgrade`; all other consumers use `.frida/contract/app/**` or read-only Frida surfaces.
Published task packs are immutable specifications; substantive changes require successor tasks and supersession tracking outside the task pack body.
Task packs MUST NOT store live execution status; normative status lives only in the task status registry.
`tasks/index.yaml` is the only normative task status registry; `tasks/sessions/**` remain append-only telemetry and MUST NOT become the status source of truth.

Task intake, task setting, task validation, and task tracking read app contract context only from `.frida/contract/app/**`; direct `.frida/inbox/app-contract/**` access is reserved for app-contract mutation interfaces.
If `.frida/contract/app/**` is missing, invalid, or stale, task validation MUST stop with `insufficient_contract_context` and MUST NOT publish a task pack.
Every published task pack MUST be backed by a machine-readable validation artifact in `.frida/reports/*.yaml`.
A task that collides with the app contract is erroneously set and MUST be rejected or rerouted rather than published with warnings.
Non-executable requests route to inbox, contract editor, architect review, or clarification before task-pack publication.
Task execution telemetry under `tasks/sessions/**` is append-only evidence and MUST NOT be edited in place.
Zone is resolved ONLY by path matching, never by profile keywords or heuristics.
Guard chain enforcement verifies structural contract conformance, not runtime behavior.
Every agent run MUST produce a job report, regardless of outcome.
FRIDA_INTERFACE_* blocks are public unless `_visibility: private` is explicitly declared.
Private interfaces MUST NOT be projected into target-repo Frida contract mirrors.
`core-tasks/` is a Frida-core-only task surface. It MUST NOT be created or read outside the Frida repo.
`tasks/` is forbidden as a task surface inside the Frida repo.
`INT_FRIDA_ZONES` is a private Frida-core-only routing block. It MUST NOT be projected into target repos.
Frida core layer files MUST use `FL##-*.yaml`; Frida-managed app layer files MUST use `AL##-*.yaml`.
`template_app_basic` MUST remain vendor-neutral and MUST NOT declare backend or implementation-specific app surfaces by default.
`template_app_basic` is a shipped derivative baseline and MUST NOT define unique normative Frida semantics absent from core contract or bootstrap policy.

Target app delivery MUST NOT expose framework authoring surfaces or source-path refs except for the public runtime config template `.frida/templates/config.template.yaml` and the app-contract SSOT authoring surface `.frida/inbox/app-contract/**` used by the public app-contract update and target toolchain-upgrade interfaces.
Public interface-instruction surfaces in target repos are seeded by Frida once and then remain user-owned in form; Frida validates only normative completeness against the backing interface.
AGENTS.md, generated router/profile surfaces, and contractual zone AGENTS.md are engine-managed invariant instruction surfaces and remain overwriteable/regenerable by Frida.
App extensions are active only when their AL## layer is linked in the target app contract index. Presence of inactive extension files alone has no effect.

FRIDA_INTERFACE_UPDATE_APP_BY_SPEC, FRIDA_INTERFACE_UPDATE_APP_BY_CODE, and FRIDA_INTERFACE_TARGET_TOOLCHAIN_UPGRADE MUST operate only within baseline or already-designed app extensions and MUST NOT invent new backend/vendor structures.
Literal paths are forbidden outside the PATHS block. Keys MUST end in *Ref.
In active app contracts, PATHS MUST store concrete filesystem facts only. Semantic roles, ownership, and lifecycle MUST live outside PATHS.
Active app contracts MUST declare PATH_SURFACES as the ownership/lifecycle companion to PATHS.
Active app contracts MUST NOT declare the same concrete filesystem path under multiple active PATHS keys.
If a top-level app entity cannot maintain a stable name -> boundary -> refs coupling, it MUST be demoted to a local/shared block or split into canonical entities.
App contracts MAY store wiki-specific app data, but FRIDA_WIKI_MECHANICS and APP_CONTRACT_SSOT remain core-owned and MUST NOT be re-declared in active app structured data.
All index documents MUST declare schema_version and it MUST equal FRIDA_SCHEMA_SUPPORT.schema_version.supported.
FRIDA_INDEX and APP_INDEX MUST use identical index format and the same schema.index/schema.assembled paths.
Index layers[].exports MUST be the authoritative registry of defined blocks (id+key+path).
Every Ref/Refs MUST resolve to exactly one exported id in the merged exports registry.
Every guards[] entry MUST conform to FRIDA_GUARD_SPEC.guard_format.
Every invariants[] entry MUST conform to FRIDA_INVARIANT_SPEC.invariant_format.
Identifiers MUST be neutral snake_case and MUST NOT contain profanity or jokes.
Contract blocks MUST NOT contain secondary migration-only id fields; use source_ref if provenance must be preserved.
Contract MUST be structured and uniform across blocks.
Guards and invariants MUST be explicit and easy to extract.
UI/CLI interfaces are out of contract scope and may evolve independently.
Component-level contracts MUST declare the full canonical component_* boundary surface.
Mounted child boundary refs and continuation/return targets MUST resolve when declared.
Root boundaries MAY omit parent_boundaryRef; non-root boundaries MUST resolve a valid parent boundary when required by the model.
If a boundary declares local_role=host_root, it MUST be the unique null-parent depth=0 anchor and all other boundaries in that app baseline MUST resolve upward to it.
mounted_child_boundaryRefs MUST be unique per boundary, MUST NOT target the current boundary, and MUST NOT create ambiguous drill-in declarations.
component_output_interface exit entries MUST declare outcomes and remain the canonical source for exit semantics.
continue requires target_boundaryRef, return requires return_target_boundaryRef, and exit forbids both.
Mounted child internals MUST remain collapsed in the parent specification view until explicit drill-in.
component_domain_blocks MUST project only through explicit projection_domains selectors on top-level child blocks; heuristics are forbidden.
component_shared_refs MUST remain dependency-only and MUST NOT redefine ownership or boundary semantics.
Authoritative component-contract examples MUST remain runtime-free; viewer/session demo state is allowed only in explicit fixture-only surfaces.
Legacy component-boundary section names are forbidden in active contract definitions unless explicitly historical/deprecated.
Component boundary semantics are the primary visual interpretation unit; YAML depth is secondary.
